checklist.detail: the project verify command was GREEN
checklist.label: Verification passed

checklist.detail: committed locally on the build branch
checklist.label: Work committed

checklist.detail: Covers all three acceptance criteria: stable contract documented, deprecation policy with staged process (announcement, grace period, removal), and changelog expectation. Minor divergence: plan specified renames with both old/new IDs served concurrently, while doc takes immutable-ID approach (IDs never change on rebrand). This still achieves the stability guarantee and is a defensible design choice, but doesn't precisely match the plan's stated mechanism.
checklist.label: Implements the approved plan

checklist.detail: Only three files changed: the new docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, a single link line in README.md, and a small 'See also' section in docs/API.md. All are documentation-only edits exactly as the plan called for. No source code touched, no unrelated churn.
checklist.label: Minimal and scoped

checklist.detail: Content is substantive: 62+ lines with a provider ID table (10+ entries), explicit stability guarantees, a 3-stage deprecation/removal policy with timeline, changelog expectations, and a FAQ section. No placeholder text, no tests weakened or deleted to force green.
checklist.label: No test-gaming / faking

checklist.detail: Documentation is accurate and poses no correctness or security risk. It sensibly describes immutable provider IDs, a 90-day deprecation window, and changelog notification expectations. No misleading or dangerous claims.
checklist.label: Safe to advance

checklist.detail: at or above the confidence bar
checklist.label: Judge confidence

dimensions.evidence: Covers all three acceptance criteria: stable contract documented, deprecation policy with staged process (announcement, grace period, removal), and changelog expectation. Minor divergence: plan specified renames with both old/new IDs served concurrently, while doc takes immutable-ID approach (IDs never change on rebrand). This still achieves the stability guarantee and is a defensible design choice, but doesn't precisely match the plan's stated mechanism.

Only three files changed: the new docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, a single link line in README.md, and a small 'See also' section in docs/API.md. All are documentation-only edits exactly as the plan called for. No source code touched, no unrelated churn.

Content is substantive: 62+ lines with a provider ID table (10+ entries), explicit stability guarantees, a 3-stage deprecation/removal policy with timeline, changelog expectations, and a FAQ section. No placeholder text, no tests weakened or deleted to force green.

Documentation is accurate and poses no correctness or security risk. It sensibly describes immutable provider IDs, a 90-day deprecation window, and changelog notification expectations. No misleading or dangerous claims.
